methodology: Cross-reference network (EXO.3.14→JHN.8.58=14v 'I AM' chain; 3 different NT contexts: apologetic/eschatological/christological) + patristic reading (Chrysostom 'Son of God called Angel, in desert NOT temple'; Haydock 'holy ground by presence'; Johnson 'I AM not I WAS'; McGarvey 'rejection→commission pattern') + CCEL (Calvin 17K lines on Ex 3, Smith acacia=tabernacle wood, Tauler mystical) + topic verification (BURNING_BUSH, MOSES, GOD, HOLINESS, ANGEL) + cross-query link to v2_hard_09 (Ex3:5→Gn28:16)

evidence: ccel_scripcom (Johnson 'I AM not I WAS — present tense proves patriarchs LIVE')
commentaries (Chrysostom 'Son of God called Angel, in desert NOT in temple', Haydock — 3 entries)

relevance_change.reason: Jesus DELIBERATELY chose the burning bush passage (not another) to prove resurrection. The argument is theologically sophisticated: 'I AM the God of Abraham' (present tense) proves the patriarchs are ALIVE. Haydock: 'doctrine of resurrection clearly in the book of Moses.'

new_refs_added.justification: 14v→JHN.8.58 ('before Abraham was, I AM'). 12v→EXO.6.3. 10v→HEB.13.8, REV.1.8. The divine self-revelation 'I AM WHO I AM' (Ehyeh Asher Ehyeh) — theological center of the burning bush theophany. The strongest christological connection in this query.

5v→JOS.5.15 and ACT.7.33. 4v→2PE.1.18. 2v→GEN.28.16 (cross-query link to Jacob's ladder!). 'Holy ground' — sanctity comes from divine PRESENCE, not location. Haydock: 'if an angel makes the place holy, how much more the incarnation of the Son!'
6v de EXO.3.6. 4v de MRK.12.26. Synoptic parallel using the same expression 'in the passage about the bush.' Links burning bush directly to resurrection argument.

candidates_noted.justification: 14v←EXO.3.14 (bidirectional with 10v). Jesus claims the 'I AM' name from the bush for himself. Christology of pre-existence.
5v→EXO.3.6. Synoptic parallel: 'He is not the God of the dead but of the living.'

three_nt_contexts.description: The burning bush is cited in 3 COMPLETELY DIFFERENT NT contexts — unique among all queries

contexts.use: Stephen retells Moses' story to show rejection→divine commission pattern (parallel Moses/Christ)
contexts.key_insight: McGarvey: 'Israel rejected Moses → God commissioned him at the bush = Israel rejected Jesus → God exalted him'

contexts.use: Jesus proves RESURRECTION using Ex 3:6 ('I AM the God of Abraham' — present tense = they LIVE now)
contexts.key_insight: Johnson: 'I AM, not I WAS — present tense implies patriarchs live now'

contexts.use: Jesus claims the divine name 'I AM' from the bush for himself — proof of DIVINITY and PRE-EXISTENCE
contexts.key_insight: EXO.3.14→JHN.8.58 = 14 votes (strongest connection in this query)

three_nt_contexts.significance_for_tcc: A search for 'sarça ardente' must retrieve verses from ALL 3 contexts. Lexical search for 'sarça' finds Ex 3 and possibly Acts 7, but completely misses Mark 12:26 (which says 'trecho referente à sarça') and John 8:58 (which doesn't mention 'sarça' at all). Semantic search must understand that the 'I AM' in Jn 8:58 echoes the 'I AM' revealed in the bush.

chrysostom_desert_not_temple.quote: Not in a temple. See how many miracles are taking place, and no word of Temple, no word of Sacrifice. And here also not simply in the desert, but in the bush.
chrysostom_desert_not_temple.significance: God encounters Moses OUTSIDE religious institutions — in a bush, in a desert. This transcends the temple-centered theology of Second Temple Judaism.

cross_query_link.connection: EXO.3.5→GEN.28.16 (2 votes) — both are theophanies where the PLACE becomes holy by divine presence. 'How awesome is this place!' (Gn 28:17) echoes 'the place where you stand is holy ground' (Ex 3:5).
cross_query_link.significance: Cross-query validation: two different queries from our set are linked by their shared theme of theophany → holy ground.
